title: 10 best herbal teas for overactive bladder
intro_desc: Herbal teas for overactive bladder are a natural and effective way to manage symptoms such as frequent urination, urgency, and incontinence. These teas work by relaxing the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing spasms, and improving overall bladder function. Examples of herbal teas that help with overactive bladder include peppermint, chamomile, and corn silk tea. Drinking these teas can improve sleep quality, reduce anxiety, and increase confidence levels, ultimately enhancing daily life for individuals with overactive bladder.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian teas helps with overactive bladder because its soothing properties can calm the urinary tract and reduce muscle contractions that lead to frequent urination. The herb's natural sedative effects also help regulate the nervous system, allowing the body to relax and reduce spasms in the bladder muscles. As a result, individuals can experience fewer urges to urinate throughout the day and night, leading to improved sleep quality and reduced stress associated with overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian root has been shown to relax the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing spasms and frequency of urination associated with an overactive bladder.

Antispasmodic properties: Valerian's antispasmodic compounds help to ease muscle contractions that can lead to frequent or sudden urges to urinate, providing relief from symptoms such as urgency and incontinence.

Urogenital relaxant effect: Valerian tea has been found to reduce the sensitivity of the urinary tract, making it less reactive to stimuli and helping to alleviate symptoms like pain or discomfort during urination.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils and sesquiterpenes, which help relax the muscles and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, a compound that has been shown to calm the nerves and reduce urinary frequency.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 teaspoons of dried valeriana officinalis root from a trusted source and store it in an airtight container.
Measure 1 teaspoon of the dried root and add it to a teapot containing 1 cup boiling water for 5 minutes steeping time.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove any loose particles and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before drinking to prevent burns from the hot liquid.
Consume the tea twice daily, morning and evening, as needed, for optimal relief from overactive bladder symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bearberry teas helps with overactive bladder because of its natural ability to reduce urinary frequency and urgency. The active compound, arbutin, in bearberry leaves has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can contribute to urinary tract infections (UTIs), a common cause of overactive bladder symptoms. Additionally, bearberry's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe the bladder muscles, reducing spasms and improving overall bladder function. This natural remedy can provide relief from frequent trips to the bathroom and alleviate the discomfort associated with an overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Ursolic acid: Helps to reduce urine production and frequency by blocking the action of certain enzymes that stimulate the bladder, leading to fewer trips to the bathroom.
Tannins: Act as a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to soothe and protect the urinary tract from irritation and infection, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
Astringent properties: Help to constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation in the urethra and bladder, reducing the sensation of urgency and frequency associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bearberry roots have been traditionally used to treat urinary issues, including frequent urination and incontinence, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Bark: The bark of the bearberry plant contains compounds that help to soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing symptoms of overactive bladder such as urgency and frequency.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried arctostaphylos uva ursi leaves and flowers for every eight ounces of water.
Combine the measured herb with boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for exactly seven minutes before straining the liquid.
Filter the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup or carafe.
Store the cooled tea in an airtight container and refrigerate for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red osier teas helps with overactive bladder because it contains a unique combination of herbal compounds that soothe and calm the urinary tract. The tea's flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce irritation and inflammation in the bladder and urethra, allowing for more efficient muscle contractions and relaxation. This helps regulate bladder function, reducing frequency and urgency of urination. Additionally, red osier teas may help strengthen the pelvic floor muscles, further alleviating overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Uric acid reduction: Red osier tea has been shown to reduce uric acid levels, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms such as frequent urination and urgency. This is because the tea contains compounds like salicylic acid and tartaric acid, which inhibit xanthine oxidase, an enzyme involved in uric acid production.

Muscle relaxant properties: The flavonoids present in red osier tea have been found to have a relaxing effect on smooth muscle, including that of the bladder and urinary tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as urgency and frequency.
Antispasmodic effects: Red osier tea's antispasmodic properties can help relieve bladder spasms and contractions, reducing the urge to urinate frequently and improving overall urinary control.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the red osier plant have been traditionally used in herbal remedies to help soothe and calm the urinary tract, making them a popular choice for treating symptoms of overactive bladder.

Barks: The barks of the red osier plant are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that can help relax muscles and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, providing relief from overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried cornus sericea leaves in the morning for optimal potency.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried cornus sericea leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large cup and discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea to taste and mix well before serving.
Consume the tea immediately after preparation or store it in an airtight container for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with overactive bladder because it contains natural astringent properties that help to soothe and calm the urinary tract. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce irritation and discomfort, while its antispasmodic effects alleviate frequent urination and urgency. Additionally, plantain's ability to strengthen the bladder muscles and improve bladder control can also help to reduce symptoms of overactive bladder. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, plantain teas provide a natural solution for managing overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Plantain tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequent urination and burning sensations.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of plantain tea can help relax the muscles in the bladder and urethra, reducing spasms and contractions that can lead to frequent urination and urge incontinence.

Astringent property: The astringent compounds in plantain tea can help reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the urinary tract, which can help alleviate symptoms such as burning sensations during urination and pelvic discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in fiber and antioxidants, plantain roots help soothe and calm urinary tract irritations associated with overactive bladder.
Barks: The bitters in plantain bark can help relax the muscles in the bladder and urethra, reducing spasms and frequency.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried leaves of plantago major and store them in airtight containers for future use.
Measure one teaspoon of dried plantago major leaves and steep them in boiling water for eight minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large cup to remove the plant material.
Allow the tea to cool slightly then mix it with cold filtered water in a ratio of 1:1 before drinking.
Consume three cups of the herbal tea two times daily for a minimum of six weeks to alleviate symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn teas helps with overactive bladder because its natural compounds, such as flavonoids and anthraquinones, have a calming effect on the urinary tract. These compounds help to soothe and tone the bladder muscles, reducing the frequency and urgency of urination. Additionally, hawthorn tea's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, hawthorn tea may provide natural relief from overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anthraquinone glycosides: These compounds help to relax the smooth muscle in the urinary tract, reducing spasms and frequency of urination.

Flavonoids: Hawthorn's flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder and urinary tract.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in hawthorn tea, including limonene and alpha-pinene, have a calming effect on the muscles of the pelvic floor, which can help to reduce urgency and frequency of urination.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the hawthorn plant have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract issues, including overactive bladder, due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The barks of the hawthorn tree contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and soothe the urinary tract, making them a popular choice for teas to alleviate overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried crataegus monogyna leaves per eight ounces of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to ten minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Add honey or lemon to taste if desired, as the bitter flavor may be unpleasant for some users.
Drink the tea three times daily, ideally at the same time each day to establish a routine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with overactive bladder because it contains a soothing gel-like substance that coats and calms the mucous membranes in the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and irritation. This natural anti-inflammatory property helps to relax the muscles in the bladder and urethra, allowing for more efficient contractions and reducing frequency and urgency of urination. Additionally, marshmallow's demulcent properties help to protect the delicate tissues in the bladder from further irritation, promoting a sense of comfort and relief for those suffering from overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Soothing anti-inflammatory effects: Marshmallow root has been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract, which can alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequent urination and discomfort.

Moisturizing demulcent properties: The gel-like substance present in marshmallow root helps to coat and protect the mucous membranes in the urinary tract, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory flavonoids: Marshmallow tea contains flavonoids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, further alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming the urinary tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
Barks: The barks contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and protect the lining of the urinary tract, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried marshmallow root extract and add it to a clean tea infuser.
Heat eight ounces of filtered water in a teapot or kettle until it reaches a gentle boil.
Pour the boiling water over the marshmallow root extract and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using the tea infuser and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, as it may help soothe the bladder muscles within 15-30 minutes of consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with overactive bladder because it has natural diuretic properties that can increase urine production, reducing the frequency of trips to the bathroom. The herb's flavonoids and taraxasterol also help relax the muscles in the urinary tract, alleviating the sudden urgency to urinate. Additionally, dandelion tea may help reduce inflammation in the bladder and urethra, further diminishing symptoms associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion tea increases urine production, which can help to reduce symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequent urination and urgency by flushing out the urinary tract.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion contains anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms such as burning or stinging sensations during urination.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds present in dandelion tea may help to relax the muscles of the bladder and urethra, reducing the frequency and intensity of involuntary contractions that can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to relax the muscles and reduce frequency of urination.
Leaves: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the urinary tract and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion leaves or use dried dandelion leaf powder in a ratio of one teaspoon per cup.
Combine the ingredients with boiling water in a teapot and let it steep for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and residue.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.
Drink two cups of cooled tea twice daily, one hour after breakfast and one hour before bedtime.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort teas helps with overactive bladder because its active compounds, hyperforin and flavonoids, have a calming effect on the muscles of the urinary tract. By relaxing the urethral sphincter and detrusor muscle, St John's Wort tea reduces muscle spasms that can cause frequent urination and incontinence. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe irritated bladder tissue, reducing discomfort and urgency associated with overactive bladder syndrome. As a natural and gentle approach, St John's Wort teas offer relief without harsh pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This active compound has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the urinary tract, which can help reduce symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequency and urgency.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort tea have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate inflammation and irritation in the bladder and urethra, contributing to improved bladder control.

Hypericum: The hypericum content in St. John's Wort tea has been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, roots are used to make medicinal teas that help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder by reducing inflammation and promoting urinary tract relaxation.
Leaves: Rich in hyperforin, leaves are used to create teas that help regulate the nervous system, reducing frequency and urgency associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves.
Heat two cups of water in a pot until boiling point is reached.
Add measured hypericum perforatum to the boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea immediately and discard any remaining liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail teas helps with overactive bladder because it is rich in silicates, which have been shown to strengthen the muscles surrounding the bladder and urethra. This can help reduce frequency and urgency of urination, as well as alleviate symptoms such as nocturia and stress urinary incontinence. Additionally, field horsetail tea has anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe the bladder lining and reduce irritation, further alleviating overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Silica content: Horsetail tea's high silica content helps to strengthen and tone the muscles in the bladder and pelvic floor, reducing frequency and urgency of urination.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in horsetail tea may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms like burning or pain during urination.
Astringent properties: Horsetail tea's astringent properties can help contract the muscles in the urinary tract and reduce muscle spasms, which can alleviate frequent and sudden urges to urinate.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silica and other minerals, field horsetail roots help strengthen the urinary tract muscles, reducing frequent urination.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioflavonoids, rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the bladder and urethra, alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried equisetum arvense plants and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Measure one teaspoon of dried equisetum arvense herb or two teaspoons of fresh leaves into a teapot.
Boil filtered water in the teapot and steep the herb for 5-7 minutes to release its bioactive compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool for 1-2 minutes before drinking it hot or warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with overactive bladder because of its natural ability to relax the muscles in the urinary tract. The herb's active compounds, such as anethole and fenchone, work to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle spasms that can lead to frequent urination. Additionally, fennel tea's soothing properties help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the bladder and urethra, allowing for more efficient bladder function and fewer episodes of overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as urinary frequency and urgency.

Muscle relaxant properties: The essential oils present in fennel tea, particularly anethole, have been shown to have muscle-relaxing effects, which can help calm the muscles in the bladder and pelvic floor, reducing spasms and involuntary contractions that contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

Antispasmodic effects: Fennel tea's antispasmodic properties can help relax smooth muscle tissues in the urinary tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms like frequent urination, nocturia, and urgency.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe bladder discomfort and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.
Fruits: Fennel fruits contain fiber, vitamins, and minerals that can help regulate bowel movements, reducing urgency and frequency associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems or use commercial supplements.
Heat filtered water in a kettle to boiling point and let it cool down for 5 minutes.
Measure out 2 teaspoons of the herb into a heat-resistant teapot or cup.
Pour the cooled-down water over the herb, steeping for 5-7 minutes before straining.
Drink the tea immediately, consuming 1 to 3 cups per day as needed or directed by a healthcare professional.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat overactive bladder with herbal teas is to combine several herbs that have a calming effect on the urinary tract. Herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and sage help relax the muscles in the bladder and reduce urgency. Drinking 2-3 cups of tea per day can significantly improve symptoms. Additionally, drinking water throughout the day helps flush out toxins and prevent irritants from accumulating in the bladder, further alleviating symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to overactive bladder that are treated with herbal teas are urinary frequency, incontinence, and irritative bladder symptoms. Herbs like Horsetail, Corn Silk, and Uva Ursi have been used traditionally to soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing symptoms of frequent urination and urgency. Additionally, herbs like Chamomile and Lavender may help relax the bladder muscles and reduce inflammation, providing relief from overactive bladder-like symptoms.
